title: Opt-in semantic search via optional peer dep + derived reconciled vectors
context: The store needed semantic (paraphrase-aware) search for better recall, but bundling @huggingface/transformers would bloat the lean install and impose setup/performance costs on users who only need fast keyword search.
decision: Made semantic search completely opt-in: @huggingface/transformers is an optional peerDependency (lazy-loaded, skipped if absent), vectors live in SQLite as a derived layer (never the source of truth), and are reconciled by content hash on every reindex to stay in sync with JSON.
consequences: Base install stays lean; no transformer overhead unless explicitly opted in.
Graceful degradation: keyword search works even if embeddings aren't available; --semantic flag fails clearly and falls back to FTS rather than silently.
Automatic vector reconciliation via content hash avoids manual sync and drift.
Users bear the cost (setup time, disk, index overhead) only if they enable semantic search.
Requires explicit `hunch embed` invocation and --semantic flag; not transparent/magic.
alternatives_rejected: Bundle transformers as a regular dependency: bloats all installs; defeats the lean-first goal.
Store embeddings in JSON alongside records: couples vectors to schema; makes migrations complex. SQLite as derived layer is cleaner.
Make semantic search required or default-on: forces setup on users who only need FTS; loses the fast path.
Embed on first query: unpredictable latency. Explicit `hunch embed` command is better UX and lets users opt-in to the cost.
Store vectors as source of truth: risks drift from JSON on reindex. Derived SQLite with JSON as authoritative is safer.
